displayTitle: The Geometry of an Honest Shadow

beats.narration: Strip away the chemistry — a radiograph is a shadow: a source, a part, a film. Geometry decides how honest that shadow is.

payload.title: THE HONEST SHADOW
payload.sub: Every radiograph is a shadow picture. Geometry keeps it true.

beats.narration: Rays spread from the source, so the shadow is always enlarged — object size times the distance ratio. Film tight against the part keeps it true.
A real source has width, so edges blur into a penumbra. Unsharpness equals source size, times object-to-film distance, divided by source-to-object distance.
Three moves shrink it: smaller source, longer source-to-object distance, shorter object-to-film distance. In the field you live by distance and film contact.
Source size was bought with the equipment. So here's the rule: long shot, film tight — set the geometry before you make the exposure.

payload.sub: Sharpness is decided before the exposure starts.
